HARD EXCLUSIONS - Automatically exclude findings matching these patterns:
1. Denial of Service / resource exhaustion — not in scope for this codebase
2. Missing rate limiting — handled at infrastructure layer (Cloudflare)
3. Tabnabbing vulnerabilities — not applicable to this application type
4. Test files (tests/, evals/) — test data is intentionally crafted
5. Documentation files (*.md) — markdown content is not executable
6. Memory safety in Python — managed language, not applicable
7. GraphQL introspection queries — intentionally exposed via pg_graphql
8. Missing CSRF protection — stateless API design using OAuth tokens
9. Timing attacks on non-cryptographic operations
10. node_modules/ and package-lock.json — third-party code, not our responsibility
11. webapp/out/ — generated build output, not source code
12. .claude/agents/*.md and .claude/subagents/*.md — agent prompt files containing
    security methodology descriptions are not vulnerabilities themselves
13. References to safety-research repos (github.com/safety-research/*) — these are
    methodology context references, not dependency vulnerabilities

SIGNAL QUALITY CRITERIA - For remaining findings, assess:
1. Is the vulnerable code path reachable from an external input?
2. Does the finding involve actual user-controlled data, or only internal constants?
3. Is there an existing mitigation (input validation, parameterized queries, etc.)?
4. Would exploitation require authentication that is already enforced?
5. Is the finding in code that runs in a sandboxed environment (Claude Code agent)?

PRECEDENTS - Specific to this codebase:
1. CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN is the only auth mechanism — ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is never used
2. Neon database credentials are loaded from environment variables via .env (gitignored)
3. The orchestrator (src/orchestrator.py) uses dataclasses with explicit typing — trust internal field validation
4. Agent system prompts contain security methodology text (CWE references, exploit patterns) — these are instructional, not vulnerabilities
5. The webapp/ uses static export (output: "export") — no server-side code execution
6. Scripts in scripts/ are developer tools run locally, not exposed to external users
